translated: Welcome to ProSeka AfterTalk for the event "Reaching Out to an Unraveling Tomorrow."
Yay!
Good evening, everyone.
I'm Hinata Sato, the voice of Mizuki Akiyama from Nightcord at 25:00.
ProSeka AfterTalk is a stream where we look back on the event while reading letters from all of you.
This time, we're focusing on "Reaching Out to an Unraveling Tomorrow."
Did you all enjoy the story?
The event just ended yesterday. How was it for everyone?
What did you think?
I hope you all enjoyed it.
It was a story where Mizuki really faced their future and thought about what they want to do.
I think it was a very meaningful chapter.
For those of you at an age where you have to decide on your education or career path, parts of this story might have really hit home.
Also, AfterTalk had a mini-renewal in April.
We've been collecting your messages in advance via the official ProSeka Discord and email.
Thank you so much to everyone who sent one in.
I'll be reading some of your letters during the show,
so please look forward to that.
If you want to post your impressions of the event or this stream,
please use the hashtag #ProSekaAfterTalk.
I'm keeping an eye on the YouTube chat and I'll check the hashtag later too,
so I'll try to pick out some interesting comments.
There are more international comments than I expected! I can't find many in Japanese. "Your ribbon is cute," "Your hair matches Mizuki's..."
The stream is probably a bit delayed for you right now,
but I see people mentioning my hair and outfit.
I tried to match the card illustration for today.
Well, I always try to, but I was especially conscious of it this time.
Good evening! Hello everyone.
Thank you all for being here.
Oh, someone said, "The story and the new song were so good I sobbed."
I'll talk about the song later.
When I saw it at home, I was like, "What is this?!"
In a good way! I was just stunned.
I really felt the incredible effort the team put into it.
Yes, exactly.
But we'll save that talk for later.
So, let's dive right into the story of "Reaching Out to an Unraveling Tomorrow."
Here we go.
We received letters about this story as well,
so I'd like to look back on the events while reading them.
[CORNER: Fan Letter Corner] First, we have a letter from Kotori no Mama.
"Hello, Hinata-san! Ko-Niigo!"
Ko-Niigo!
"My child and I enjoy playing ProSeka together."
"Mizuki-chan's banner was incredibly cute, interesting, and moving."
"Through their past experiences and interactions with those around them,"
"I'm glad we got to see so many of Mizuki-chan's different feelings."
"I think staying true to yourself and not giving up on what you love can be hard when you face obstacles."
"But I believe that if you hold onto those feelings, a time will come when they are rewarded."
"Just like the members of Niigo, there are definitely people out there"
"who will see and accept you for who you truly are."
"I really love the stories in ProSeka."
"Hinata-san, thank you so much for always voicing them."
"I'll continue to support both you and Mizuki-chan with all my heart."
Thank you so much, Kotori no Mama.
In this story, Mizuki's sister comes home from abroad.
It starts with the pop-up shop, and I felt it was a huge step forward for Mizuki to want their sister to meet the rest of Niigo.
Kanade and Mafuyu had met Yuuki-san before,
but Ena met her for the first time here. The story really progressed in many ways.
There was a lot of development.
Mizuki loves making videos, but they also love things like
remaking clothes, visiting boutiques, and looking at makeup. They have so many things they love.
So, when it comes to what they want to do in the future, thinking about what they want to aim for...
I think it's amazing that they're already facing those questions at this stage.
When I was going to college, I just thought that if I was paying for it,
I had to study something that would give me a practical skill. I spent all my time at open campuses thinking like that.
So, my approach to finding what I liked was completely different from Mizuki's.
Even so, I think this was a time for Mizuki to re-examine what they love,
at their own pace. That's such an important thing to do.
It wasn't just about their sister's pop-up shop.
Being around those clothes helped them realize that it's not just a hobby—they might want to make it their career.
Realizing that is a huge deal, so I was just really happy to see them smiling.
